column2: # Step 1: Load the two files containing TYP and ProcP durations
typ_data <- read.csv("gaps_p2c.csv")  # Replace with your actual file path for TYP
procp_data <- read.csv("gaps_c2p.csv")  # Replace with your actual file path for ProcP
# Step 2: Calculate the average duration by participant (filename), condition, and engagement level for both files
group_by(filename, condition, eng_level) %>%  # Group by filename, condition, and engagement level
summarise(avg_duration = mean(gap_p2c_duration, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%  # Calculate average duration
mutate(event_type = "TYP")  # Add a column to indicate this is TYP data
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'filename', 'condition'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
group_by(filename, condition, eng_level) %>%  # Group by filename, condition, and engagement level
summarise(avg_duration = mean(gaps_c2p_duration, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%  # Calculate average duration
mutate(event_type = "ProcP")  # Add a column to indicate this is ProcP data
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'filename', 'condition'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
# Step 3: Combine the TYP and ProcP data
# Rename engagement levels and conditions for consistency
eng_level = factor(eng_level, levels = c("EC", "AL", "PL")),  # Reorder engagement levels
condition = factor(condition, levels = c("Human-Human", "Human-Robot"))  # Ensure correct order
group_by(event_type, condition, eng_level) %>%  # Group by filename, condition, and engagement level
summarise(avg_duration = mean(avg_duration, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%  # Calculate average duration
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'event_type', 'condition'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
